llm_taxonomy.evidence_summary: Stored evidence describes Taito Milestones 4 as a retro arcade compilation containing ten different games across multiple distinct genres and play styles: sports minigames, martial arts/fighting, block-breaking/paddle action, platform action, shoot 'em up, maze/ball-rotation play, and beat-'em-up style action. The included games do not converge on a single stable gameplay identity that maps cleanly to one Similar Games Taxonomy V2 archetype. The Metacritic description is also contradictory/noisy, referring to an unrelated puzzle game ('ZOK'), which further weakens title-level archetype assignment. Because this is a mixed-genre compilation rather than one coherent game loop, classification into a single archetype would be misleading.

resolved_similar_games.why_similar: Direct neighboring entry in the same Taito arcade compilation series, with the same curated multi-genre retro collection format.
Another installment built around a bundle of classic Taito arcade games spanning shooters, action, and other short-session coin-op styles.

The core loop is beat-synced traversal through maze-like levels, solving puzzles, battling security programs, and defeating major AI boss encounters. This strongly supports rhythm_game as the primary identity, with the rhythm-timing input layer driving both traversal and combat across a single-player campaign structure.

llm_taxonomy.evidence_summary: Stored evidence consistently describes a retro-inspired collection of 26 remastered Neopets mini-games with Arcade/Challenge/Endless modes, high-score chasing, unlockables, and some local multiplayer. However, the bundle is intentionally heterogeneous and the text does not establish a single dominant gameplay identity that cleanly maps to one allowed Similar Games Taxonomy V2 archetype. The current computed signals that inferred first-person shooter traits are clearly spurious relative to the actual description. 'Party game' is the closest possible fit, but the evidence emphasizes an arcade mini-game compilation with mostly solo/high-score play rather than a clearly social, match-driven party-game structure. Because the package is multi-mode and cross-genre without a strong singular archetype in the allowed list, classification should be rejected rather than forced.

resolved_similar_games.why_similar: Fast, varied mini-game-driven play built around short sessions, replayability, and local multiplayer party appeal.
Arcade-style challenge structure focused on brief runs, score/time optimization, and replaying classic-style activities.
Retro compilation format with many distinct arcade-style games, modernized presentation, and high-score chasing.
Accessible classic arcade collection centered on short-session gameplay, recognizable variety, and leaderboard-style replay value.
Curated retro collection that modernizes a library of older games for contemporary play while preserving pick-up-and-play arcade structure.

llm_taxonomy.evidence_summary: Classification is supported primarily by explicit source labeling and the strongly story-led synopsis. Metacritic directly labels the game as a 'Visual Novel,' which is the clearest gameplay-identity signal in the bundle. The stored descriptions focus on protagonist, romance, betrayal, and outcome/fate rather than action systems, traversal, or combat mechanics, which fits a narrative-first visual novel structure. The current taxonomy debug payload also already extracted 'combat_presence: none' and 'narrative_structure: authored_linear' from stored evidence, reinforcing a non-combat, linear story experience. 'Otome' framing and the romance-heavy language support interpersonal drama within an anime-styled single-player campaign format. Some fingerprint fields remain inferred at low granularity because concrete UI/mechanical details are sparse, but the core archetype is well supported.

llm_taxonomy.evidence_summary: Stored descriptions consistently frame the game around skill-based block-breaking 'Rebound challenges' where the player keeps a walnut in play, manipulates shot angles with timed tail-whips, clears every block from stages, chases long volleys/high scores, and uses power-up interactions across varied obstacle layouts. That strongly supports a pinball-like arcade core rather than RPG or sports identity. The same copy also describes an interconnected six-floor tower between challenge stages, with environmental puzzles, unlocked paths, NPC help requests, secrets, bosses, and a linear mystery campaign, which supports metroidvania as a secondary influence rather than the primary loop. Evidence is solid from repeated store descriptions, but some specifics like exact camera/perspective and traversal verbs are less explicit, so confidence is moderate-high rather than maximal.

llm_taxonomy.evidence_summary: The DLC/bundle title itself provides no standalone gameplay description, but DLC guidance applies and the parent game has strong curated gameplay evidence. Super Mario Bros. Wonder is explicitly described as 'classic Mario side-scrolling gameplay' with courses, platforming-focused progression, power-ups, enemies, and local co-op. That strongly supports action_platformer over precision_platformer: the core identity is broad Mario-style stage clearing and platform navigation rather than ultra-strict fail-heavy precision challenges. 'Meetup in Bellabel Park' appears additive and non-contradictory, so the parent game's core gameplay identity is inherited for this Switch 2 Edition bundle.

llm_taxonomy.evidence_summary: Stored descriptions consistently frame Copa City as a football-event tycoon/management game rather than a play-on-the-pitch sports title: 'economic strategy,' 'first football tycoon,' 'build in the city,' 'manage its structures and stadium,' and provide 'entertainment, food, and security' for fans, with success measured by 'Match Readiness.' The player organizes high-stakes football events, prepares urban space and stadium infrastructure, and manages club-specific fan behaviors and safety/fun tradeoffs. This strongly supports a management/tycoon identity with city-building and simulation strategy elements, using soccer as the theme rather than the primary play loop. Current taxonomy hints toward sports_sim are explained by football/match terminology, but the concrete gameplay loop is build/manage/optimize operations around matches, not directly competing in them.

resolved_similar_games.why_similar: Management-heavy crowd-flow and venue operations sim focused on building facilities, services, and guest satisfaction around large entertainment events.
City-building and infrastructure planning overlap closely with preparing urban space, traffic, services, and public logistics for big matchdays.
The clearest football-management comp, though it centers on club operations and squad decisions rather than stadium-area event logistics.
Operations-focused tycoon about designing infrastructure, managing passenger flow, security, and service bottlenecks in a high-throughput venue.
Accessible management sim built around placing facilities, staffing support services, and optimizing readiness and visitor/student needs across a themed campus.

llm_taxonomy.evidence_summary: Stored descriptions consistently present Étrange Overlord as a frenetic real-time action RPG: players 'shoot, dodge, and slash' through battlefields, use a unique revolving-lane combat mechanic, and command from a roster of 15+ playable allies in 2-4 player co-op. That supports a combat-dominant, fast, stage-oriented action identity more than RPG subgenres centered on loot, open worlds, or party command from an overhead perspective. The text includes musical framing and songs, but it does not clearly establish rhythm-timing as the core gameplay loop; the current computed rhythm/party signals appear driven by thematic wording rather than concrete mechanics. Overall the strongest fit is character_action, with some beat-em-up overlap due to multiplayer crowd-fighting emphasis.

Because the core gameplay identity is clear but falls into a taxonomy gap rather than an existing supported archetype, this title should be rejected instead of misclassified.

It suggests a hybrid of horror action, survival, puzzle-solving, and roguelike structure, but key archetype-defining signals are missing or contradictory: no reliable perspective/view, no clear combat model beyond trap use and occasional empowered state, and no strong fit to survival_horror, action_horror, psychological_horror, turn_based_tactics, or immersive_sim_action as defined by the available taxonomy. Because the gameplay identity appears materially hybrid and the closest archetypes are only partial matches, classification would be too speculative.

llm_taxonomy.evidence_summary: Stored descriptions consistently identify the game as an action-adventure centered on exploration plus highly technical twin-stick shooting, alternating between an exploration phase and a home-base upgrade/shop phase, with weapon pickups, skill customization, boss battles, and optional local co-op. However, that gameplay identity does not cleanly fit the allowed archetype set: it is not a beat 'em up, and the evidence is too mixed for open_world_action_adventure because the copy also frames progression around clearing stages/areas and boss encounters rather than a clearly quest-web open world structure. The result is a strong description of the game, but not a strong enough match to an existing taxonomy archetype.

resolved_similar_games.why_similar: Twin-stick shooting, exploration-driven zones, lootable weapons, character upgrades, and co-op-friendly combat progression.

title: Overpowered 2 - Crux of Fate
anchor_summary: A top-down survivors-like arena shooter: dodge swarming monsters, auto-fire or passive weapons, collect XP gems, level up into a bigger arsenal, and rely on repeated runs with gradual improvement.

similar_games.title: Vampire Survivors
similar_games.why_similar: Defines the horde-survival loop of movement-focused dodging, automatic attacks, XP pickups, mid-run weapon upgrades, and escalating enemy waves.

similar_games.title: Overpowered
similar_games.why_similar: The prior game in the same series is the clearest direct comp if this sequel keeps the same top-down arena survival and weapon-stacking structure.

similar_games.title: Brotato
similar_games.why_similar: Another compact arena survivor built around surviving timed waves, stacking weapons, and making rapid build choices between runs.

similar_games.title: Soulstone Survivors
similar_games.why_similar: Shares the auto-attacking bullet-heaven format with large enemy hordes, frequent level-up choices, and run-based power growth.

similar_games.title: Halls of Torment
similar_games.why_similar: Very similar top-down retro-styled survival combat with kiting, auto-triggered offense, XP collection, and surviving until wave objectives are cleared.

llm_taxonomy.evidence_summary: Stored store descriptions consistently identify a top-down retro pixel-art single-player survival shooter focused on surviving enemy waves in a confined arena, collecting XP drops, auto-attacking with acquired weapons, and gaining stronger odds through repeated failed attempts. This is strong evidence for the game's gameplay identity, but that identity is a survivorlike / bullet-heaven style auto-attacking horde survival game, which is not represented by the allowed Similar Games Taxonomy V2 archetype list. Available archetypes such as arena_fps, loot_shooter, action_horror, co_op_action_roguelite, or extraction_shooter would materially misclassify the core loop.

resolved_similar_games.why_similar: Defines the horde-survival loop of movement-focused dodging, automatic attacks, XP pickups, mid-run weapon upgrades, and escalating enemy waves.
The prior game in the same series is the clearest direct comp if this sequel keeps the same top-down arena survival and weapon-stacking structure.

title: Ariana and the Elder Codex
anchor_summary: 2D action-platformer centered on combat-and-platforming challenge rooms, boss fights, and ability-based progression, with a strong emphasis on unlocking and loadout-customizing magic skills that open new paths and improve survivability.

similar_games.title: Shantae and the Seven Sirens
similar_games.why_similar: Side-scrolling action-platforming with magic ability unlocks, backtracking to newly reachable areas, and a light, character-driven adventure structure.

similar_games.title: Ender Lilies: Quietus of the Knights
similar_games.why_similar: 2D combat-platformer built around collecting abilities, equipping different skill loadouts, tough bosses, and ability-gated progression.

similar_games.why_similar: Anime-styled 2D action-platforming with precision boss fights, magic-focused combat, and exploration that expands through newly gained movement/combat powers.

similar_games.title: Dust: An Elysian Tail
similar_games.why_similar: Fast 2D action-platforming with RPG-style stat growth, crafted/equipped upgrades, and combat-heavy progression through interconnected areas.

similar_games.title: Rabi-Ribi
similar_games.why_similar: Bullet-heavy 2D action-platformer featuring demanding boss encounters, movement upgrades, and progression shaped by platforming skill and character build choices.

llm_taxonomy.evidence_summary: Stored gameplay text is sufficient and consistent for a metroidvania classification. The Steam detailed description explicitly describes a 2D platformer structure with combat and ability-gated exploration: Ariana defeats enemies to earn new magic spells and abilities that grant access to previously unreachable areas, takes on platforming and combat challenges, and fights formidable bosses. This strongly supports side-scrolling traversal, platform navigation, boss-driven combat, and progression via new abilities opening the map over time, which is the core identity of metroidvania. The seven codices framing suggests segmented but exploratory spaces rather than purely linear stages, so semi_open is the best fit. Secondary action_platformer is also supported, but the explicit unreachable-area gating and ability unlock loop make metroidvania the stronger primary archetype.

resolved_similar_games.why_similar: Side-scrolling action-platforming with magic ability unlocks, backtracking to newly reachable areas, and a light, character-driven adventure structure.
